seoTitle: Ivanhoe East vs Jacana — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Ivanhoe East vs Jacana: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ivanhoe East and Jacana.
heading: Ivanhoe East vs Jacana — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ivanhoe East, Victoria and Jacana, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ivanhoe East has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Jacana (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ivanhoe East or Jacana safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ivanhoe East has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Jacana scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ivanhoe East scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Jacana scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ivanhoe East or Jacana?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ivanhoe East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
